name: Pediatrix Medical Group, Inc.
summary: Pediatrix Medical Group, Inc. is a leading provider of physician services specializing in newborn, maternal-fetal, and pediatric subspecialty care. Its national network of approximately 2,295 affiliated physicians operates in 37 states, staffing over 360 NICUs, approximately 60 PICUs, and over 50 hospitals. Services include neonatal care, maternal-fetal medicine, pediatric intensive care, pediatric hospitalist care, pediatric surgery, and newborn hearing screening at 340 hospitals, screening over 793,000 babies annually.

products.name: Neonatal Intensive Care (NICU) Staffing and Management
products.description: 24/7 staffing and management of NICUs at over 360 hospitals in 32 states by affiliated neonatologists and nurse practitioners.

products.name: Maternal-Fetal Medicine
products.description: Inpatient and office-based clinical care for high-risk expectant mothers and their unborn babies by affiliated subspecialists.

products.name: Pediatric Intensive Care (PICU) Staffing
products.description: Staffing and management of PICUs at approximately 60 hospitals by affiliated pediatric intensivists.

products.name: Pediatric Hospitalist Services
products.description: Inpatient pediatric and newborn care in PICUs, well-born nurseries, and pediatric emergency rooms at over 50 hospitals.

products.name: Pediatric Surgery Services
products.description: Specialized surgical care for newborns to adolescents, including neonatal, trauma, oncology, and craniofacial surgery.

products.name: Newborn Hearing Screening Program
products.description: Screening for potential hearing loss at 340 hospitals; screened over 793,000 babies in 2025; launched in 1994.

products.name: Newborn Nursery Program
products.description: In-hospital nursery care and evaluation for healthy newborns during their hospital stay, provided by affiliated clinicians.

products.name: Clinical Research and Quality Initiatives
products.description: Physician-led clinical research, outcomes monitoring, and continuing medical education focused on women's and children's services.

partnerships.description: Contracts with hospitals to staff and manage NICUs, PICUs, and other pediatric units across 37 states.
